name: Агитплакат восходящего солнца — Наруто Узумаги
moment: Naruto Uzumaki in Sage Mode / Hokage-cloak silhouette, full figure, 82% of frame height, heroic socialist-realism poster pose — standing wide-legged on a pedestal built from a shattered stone monument (a cracked half of a Hokage-rock slab and a broken masked fragment lying at his feet), one hand held low forming a Rasengan (a compact swirling blue-white chakra sphere cupped in his palm, rendered as a flat graphic rosette rather than a painterly effect), the other arm raised overhead with a clenched fist, his short spiky blond hair catching the light, orange-and-black jumpsuit with the Uzumaki spiral on the back visible at the shoulder, the Konoha forehead protector plate glinting on his brow, three whisker-like scar lines on each cheek clearly visible, sage-mode pigmentation (orange eye markings) may tint the eyes — behind him a large graphic disc of a rising sun with sharp straight sunburst rays, and within the disc a bold flat silhouette of the Nine-Tails fox (Kurama) rearing up on its hind legs, nine tails fanned out symmetrically framing the sun-disc's upper arc.
art_style: японский агитационный плакат 1930-40х в переработке под аниме-целл: плоские насыщенные заливки без градиентов, жёсткие геометричные лучи солнца, лёгкая печатная зернистость и минимальное смещение цветных слоёв как на старой литографии, драматичный ракурс снизу
typography: Main phrase 'NEVER GIVE UP' set in a heavy geometric propaganda-poster slab display face (blocky, high-contrast stroke, slightly condensed like Cyrillic-constructivist posters), fill solid flat burnt-orange with a thin cream-white keyline (0.03x cap-height), positioned in a straight horizontal band across the upper third of the canvas directly in front of the sun-disc rays but on the topmost layer (rays visible only around the letters, never behind them as a texture-fill), cap-height 0.135x figure width, word gap 0.05x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. Secondary line 'HOKAGE'S WILL' set in a smaller matching slab weight directly below the main phrase, stacked with a line-gap of 1.1x cap-height, cap-height 0.06x figure width, fill flat cream-white with a thin burnt-orange keyline (inverse of the main line for poster-contrast rhythm), word gap 0.045x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. The vertical kanji/katakana column ナルト (Naruto, his given name) is placed inside the lower-left margin beside Naruto's leg, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat-crimson stamp-style column matching the poster's stencil character, glyph size 0.08x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: burnt orange, cream off-white, deep charcoal-black, crimson red accent, golden-yellow sun-disc, cobalt-blue Rasengan glow

name: Агитплакат восходящего солнца — Итачи Учиха (тёмная версия)
moment: Itachi Uchiha full figure, 82% of frame height, heroic-but-somber socialist-realism poster pose — standing wide-legged on a pedestal built from a shattered stone monument (a cracked half of an Uchiha clan-fan crest slab and a broken masked fragment lying at his feet), one hand resting near his hip on a kunai holster, the other arm raised overhead with a clenched fist, long black hair tied back with loose strands framing his face, his black Akatsuki cloak with large red cloud motifs draped over his shoulders and billowing symmetrically like wings, his Konoha forehead protector visible with a single diagonal scratch through the leaf-symbol (missing-nin mark), his Sharingan eyes glowing crimson with visible tomoe, two long thin dark tear-trough lines running from his lower eyelids down his cheeks — behind him a large graphic disc of a rising sun rendered in inverted black-and-crimson (a black solar disc with sharp straight crimson-red sunburst rays styled to echo the Akatsuki cloud pattern), and within the disc a bold flat silhouette of three ravens in flight, wings spread, arranged in a loose ascending diagonal across the upper arc of the disc.
art_style: японский агитационный плакат 1930-40х в переработке под аниме-целл, но в мрачной инверсированной палитре: плоские насыщенные заливки без градиентов, жёсткие геометричные лучи солнца выполнены как облака Акацуки, лёгкая печатная зернистость и минимальное смещение цветных слоёв как на старой литографии, драматичный ракурс снизу, общий тон тяжелее и мрачнее эталона
typography: Main phrase 'PEACE THROUGH SACRIFICE' set in a heavy geometric propaganda-poster slab display face (blocky, high-contrast stroke, slightly condensed like Cyrillic-constructivist posters), fill solid flat crimson-red with a thin charcoal-black keyline (0.03x cap-height), positioned in a straight horizontal band across the upper third of the canvas directly in front of the black sun-disc rays but on the topmost layer (rays visible only around the letters, never behind them as a texture-fill); because the phrase is three words it is set on a single line at a slightly reduced cap-height of 0.10x figure width to keep all three words on one row without crowding, word gap 0.045x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. Secondary line 'FOREVER IN THE SHADOWS' set in a smaller matching slab weight directly below the main phrase, stacked with a line-gap of 1.1x cap-height, cap-height 0.05x figure width, fill flat cream-white (the only warm-light accent on an otherwise dark poster) with a thin crimson keyline, word gap 0.04x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. The vertical katakana column イタチ (Itachi, his given name) is placed inside the lower-left margin beside his leg, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat-crimson stamp-style column matching the poster's stencil character, glyph size 0.08x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: deep black, crimson red, charcoal grey, cream off-white accent, dark maroon shadow tone

name: Агитплакат восходящего солнца — Пейн (Яхико-Пейн)
moment: Pain (the Yahiko-body Deva Path) full figure, 82% of frame height, cold authoritarian socialist-realism poster pose — standing wide-legged on a pedestal built from a shattered stone monument (a cracked half of the Amegakure headband-plate slab and a broken masked fragment lying at his feet), one hand held out low in front, palm open in the classic Shinra Tensei gesture, the other arm raised overhead with a clenched fist, short spiky orange-red hair, pale skin, his Rinnegan eyes rendered clearly with concentric ripple-ring pupils, multiple straight black piercing-rods studding his nose, ears and lower lip in the canon pattern, his black Akatsuki cloak with red cloud motifs draped over his shoulders and billowing symmetrically like wings, his Amegakure forehead protector visible with a single diagonal scratch through the symbol (missing-nin mark) — behind him a large graphic disc of a rising sun rendered in muted grey-orange (a slate-grey solar disc with sharp straight burnt-orange sunburst rays), thin diagonal rain-streak hatching etched across the rays and disc surface evoking the eternal rain of Amegakure, and within the disc a row of six bold flat silhouettes (the Six Paths of Pain) standing shoulder to shoulder in identical stance along the upper arc of the disc.
art_style: японский агитационный плакат 1930-40х в переработке под аниме-целл, приглушённая дождливая палитра: плоские насыщенные заливки без градиентов (кроме тонкой штриховки дождя), жёсткие геометричные лучи солнца, лёгкая печатная зернистость и минимальное смещение цветных слоёв как на старой литографии, драматичный ракурс снизу, общий тон холодный и монументальный
typography: Main phrase 'KNOW PAIN' set in a heavy geometric propaganda-poster slab display face (blocky, high-contrast stroke, slightly condensed like Cyrillic-constructivist posters), fill solid flat burnt-orange with a thin slate-grey keyline (0.03x cap-height), positioned in a straight horizontal band across the upper third of the canvas directly in front of the sun-disc rays but on the topmost layer (rays visible only around the letters, never behind them as a texture-fill), cap-height 0.135x figure width, word gap 0.05x cap-height, each word appears exactly once. Secondary line 'FEEL THE WORLD'S PAIN' set in a smaller matching slab weight directly below the main phrase, stacked with a line-gap of 1.1x cap-height, cap-height 0.05x figure width, fill flat cream-white with a thin burnt-orange keyline (inverse of the main line for poster-contrast rhythm), word gap 0.045x cap-height, each word appears exactly once; note the word PAIN appears once in the main line and once again in the secondary line — this is intentional (two distinct sentences, not a duplication error), no word repeats more than once within either individual line. The vertical katakana column ペイン (Pain, his title) is placed inside the lower-left margin beside his leg, outside the figure's silhouette, rendered as a bold flat-orange stamp-style column matching the poster's stencil character, glyph size 0.08x figure width, one clean unbroken column of exactly three glyphs, gap between glyphs 0.04x glyph height, appears exactly once. No other text anywhere on the print.
palette: slate grey, burnt orange, cream off-white, deep charcoal-black, muted rain-blue hatching accent
